revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23993722414|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:神長官守矢史料館]] {{Infobox Museum | Name = Jinchōkan Moriya Shiryōkan | image = [[File:神長官守矢史料館 (51790275588).jpg|280px]]
View of the museum from the east (July 2021) [[File:Jinchokan Moriya Museum.jpg|280px]] | Caption = View of the museum from the southeast (August 2009) {{Maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|type=point|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|frame-height=210|coord={{Coord|35|59|43.9|N|138|7|40.8|E|}}|marker=museum|frame-latitude=36.006|frame-longitude=138.121}} | image_map = 1 | Official Name = Chino City Jinchōkan Moriya Shiryōkan[http://www.chinoshi.jp/reiki/ Ordinance of Chino City Jinchōkan Moriya Shiryōkan] | Nickname = | Former Name = | Specialization = Moriya Family Documents, {{ill|Suwa Region|ja|諏訪地域|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Region|qq=}} {{ill|Local history|en|Local history|qid=Q1160621|lt=Local history|1=Local history}} | Organization = Chino City | Management = Chino City Board of Education | Annual Budget = | Total Floor Area = | Research Staff = | Opened = | Closed = | Address Postal Code = 391-0013 | Address = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|qid=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture|1=Nagano Prefecture}} {{ill|Chino, Nagano|en|Chino, Nagano|qid=Q838660|lt=Chino City|1=Chino, Nagano}} {{ill|Miyakawa (Chino City)|ja|宮川 (茅野市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyakawa (Chino City)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮川村 (長野県)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮川村 (長野県)|1=Miyagawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17176944}} 389-1 | Latitude = 35| Latitude minutes = 59| Latitude seconds = 43.9 | Longitude = 138| Longitude minutes = 7| Longitude seconds = 40.8 | Map Country Code = JP-20 | Website = [https://www.city.chino.lg.jp/ Official Chino City Website] }} '''Jinchōkan Moriya Shiryōkan''' (神長官守矢史料館) is a {{ill|Museum|en|Museum|qid=Q33506|lt=museum|1=Museum}} located in {{ill|Miyakawa (Chino City)|ja|宮川 (茅野市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyakawa (Chino City)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮川村 (長野県)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮川村 (長野県)|1=Miyagawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17176944}}, {{ill|Chino, Nagano|en|Chino, Nagano|qid=Q838660|lt=Chino City|1=Chino, Nagano}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|qid=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture|1=Nagano Prefecture}}, Japan. The museum preserves and displays documents related to the Moriya family, which served as {{ill|Kancho|ja|神長官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kancho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Shinto ritual officials) for the {{ill|Suwa-taisha|en|Suwa-taisha|qid=Q218813|lt=Suwa-taisha|1=Suwa-taisha}} Upper Shrine until the early {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}. It is situated within the grounds of the Moriya family residence. == History == * In {{ill|1989|en|1989|qid=Q2425|lt=1989|1=1989}}, the 78th head of the Moriya family, {{ill|Moriya Sanae|ja|守矢早苗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya Sanae|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and Chino City requested the design of the museum from architect {{ill|Terunobu Fujimori|en|Terunobu Fujimori|qid=Q3134309|lt=Terunobu Fujimori|1=Terunobu Fujimori}}. Construction was completed in {{ill|1991|en|1991|qid=Q2062|lt=Heisei 3|1=1991}}.Fujimori's design for the building was initially titled 'Museum of Moriya.' * The first director was {{ill|Kosuke Hosoda|ja|細田貴助|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosuke Hosoda|1=Kisuke Hosoda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q63958829}}, a local historian and former principal of {{ill|Ochiai Elementary School|ja|富士見町立富士見小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochiai Elementary School|1=Fujimi Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11456192}} in {{ill|Fujimi, Nagano|en|Fujimi, Nagano|qid=Q1204145|lt=Fujimi Town|1=Fujimi, Nagano}}. He served until 1995. * The current director is Tamura Ken. == Collection == The museum holds significant historical documents, including: * {{ill|Suwa Daimyōjin Ekotoba|en|Suwa Daimyōjin Ekotoba|qid=Q11631818|lt=Suwa Daimyōjin Ekotoba|1=Suwa Daimyōjin Ekotoba}} * {{ill|Nenai Shinji Shidai Kyūki|ja|年内神事次第旧記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nenai Shinji Shidai Kyūki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Gofureisho|ja|御符礼書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gofureisho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Additionally, it houses letters related to famous samurai such as [[Takeda Shingen]]{{efn|Japanese feudal lord (1521–1573)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Shingen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Takeda Nobumasa|ja|武田信昌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Nobumasa|1=Takeda Nobumasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q6640068}}, {{ill|Takeda Nobunawa|ja|武田信縄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Nobunawa|1=Takeda Nobutsuna|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q6639945}}, {{ill|Murakami Yoshikiyo|en|Murakami Yoshikiyo|qid=Q1048622|lt=Murakami Yoshikiyo|1=Murakami Yoshikiyo}}, and {{ill|Sanada Masayuki|en|Sanada Masayuki|qid=Q1144171|lt=Sanada Masayuki|1=Sanada Masayuki}}. == Lobby Exhibition Room == The lobby features a recreation of offerings for the {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御頭祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onbashira Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, including a deer head. == Permanent Exhibition Room == The permanent exhibition showcases letters and excavated artifacts. These displays are part of rotating exhibits that change every few months. For information on current exhibits, refer to the links below. == Architecture == The building, designed by architect {{ill|Terunobu Fujimori|en|Terunobu Fujimori|qid=Q3134309|lt=Terunobu Fujimori|1=Terunobu Fujimori}}, is a fireproof reinforced concrete structure. It incorporates natural and local materials, including: * {{ill|Teppeiseki|ja|鉄平石|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Teppeiseki|1=Q11649551|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11649551}} roofing, a traditional technique revived after 50 years * Siding made of split cypress boards using traditional methods * Interior elements such as straw-infused mortar and hand-blown glass Despite its revival of lost traditions, the structure avoids simple historicism, creating a unique and rooted design."End of Architecture / Beginning of Architecture" (INAX Publishing, 2001) Nearby, visitors can view other works by Fujimori, including {{ill|Takasugi-an|ja|高過庵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasugi-an|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Hikusugi-an|ja|低過庵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikusugi-an|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Flying Mud Boat|ja|空飛ぶ泥舟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flying Mud Boat|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, though they are generally not open to the public. == Grounds == * '''Gosakuchi Shrine''': A central site of {{ill|Mishaguji|en|Mishaguji|qid=Q11071209|lt=Mishaguji|1=Mishaguji}} worship. Its forest is a designated natural monument of Chino City. * '''Kaidōden (Prayer Hall)''': Historically used for oral transmission of family secrets, including rituals and genealogies. * '''Jinchōkan Kofun''': A 7th-century tumulus with a diameter of approximately 9.5m. Artifacts include wooden coffin fragments, swords, and pottery.{{Cite web|url=https://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=1510&seq=0|title=Jinchōkan Kofun|website=82 Cultural Properties Foundation}} * '''Ōhōri Suwa Family Cemetery''': A burial site for the Suwa family that later became a communal graveyard. == Notes == For details on opening hours, holidays, and admission fees, refer to the links below. == Access == * About 40 minutes on foot (2.7 km) from {{ill|Chino Station|en|Chino Station|qid=Q844063|lt=Chino Station|1=Chino Station}} ({{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|qid=Q499071|lt=JR East|1=East Japan Railway Company}}, Chūō Main Line) * About 12 minutes on foot (900 m) from "Kami-sha" bus stop on {{ill|Suwa Bus Aruga-Kamisha Route|ja|諏訪バス 有賀・上社統合路線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Bus Aruga-Kamisha Route|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * About 30 minutes on foot (2.2 km) from "Suwa Interchange" bus stop on {{ill|Chūō Expressway Bus Suwa-Okaya Line|ja|中央高速バス 諏訪・岡谷線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūō Expressway Bus Suwa-Okaya Line|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == See Also == * {{ill|Miyakawa Village (Nagano)|ja|宮川村 (長野県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyakawa Village (Nagano)|1=Miyagawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17176944}} * {{ill|Suwa Taisha Upper Shrine|ja|諏訪大社#前宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Taisha Upper Shrine|1=Suwa-taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q218813}} * {{ill|Ankokuji (Chino City)|ja|安国寺 (茅野市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ankokuji (Chino City)|1=Ankokoji (Chino City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q20043040}} * {{ill|Battle of Miyakawa|ja|宮川の戦い|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Miyakawa|1=Battle of Miyagawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17214872}} * {{ill|Suwa City Museum|ja|諏訪市博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa City Museum|1=Suwa City Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q85883757}} * {{ill|Vernacular architecture|en|Vernacular architecture|qid=Q930314|lt=Vernacular architecture|1=Vernacular architecture}} * {{ill|Critical regionalism|en|Critical regionalism|qid=Q24798|lt=Critical regionalism|1=Critical regionalism}} * {{ill|Tourist attractions in Chino City|ja|茅野市#観光スポット|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tourist attractions in Chino City|1=Chino, Nagano|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q838660}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|editor=Chino City Jinchōkan Moriya Shiryōkan|year=2010|title=Guidebook to Jinchōkan Moriya Shiryōkan and Surrounding Area}} * {{Cite book|editor=Chino City Jinchōkan Moriya Shiryōkan|year=2017|title=Jinchōkan Moriya Shiryōkan Pamphlet|edition=3rd edition}} * {{Cite book|author=Kosuke Hosoda|year=2003|title=Reading the Moriya Documents of Nagano Prefecture|publisher=Hoozuki Books|isbn=978-4-434-03552-4}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.chino.lg.jp/soshiki/bunkazai/1639.html Jinchōkan Moriya Shiryōkan Official Website] * [https://www.city.chino.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/8154.pdf Moriya Family Documents Catalog (PDF)] * [https://www.nagano-museum.com/info/detail.php?fno=135&tn=1 Nagano Museum Guide] * [http://www.chinoshiminkan.jp/cmm2021/ Town Museum Website] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{translated page|ja|神長官守矢史料館|version=101142083|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:神長官守矢史料館]]
